title: Speak Up (Feedback + Complaints)
content: dSpeak Up
dHow to tell DYAR what you think
d[HEADER-IMAGE: Person speaking into a megaphone with a speech bubble ? bright and friendly]
dDYAR wants to hear from you
d[IMAGE: Ear icon with sound waves coming in]
dTell us when things are GOOD.
dTell us when things are NOT good.
dTell us what could be BETTER.
dIt is OK to speak up. You will NOT get in trouble. Your supports will NOT stop.
d ways to speak up to DYAR
d[IMAGE: Four icons in a row: phone, envelope (email), pen + paper, person-to-person speech bubble]
d. Phone
d[ICON: Phone icon]
dCall Mehdi on 0470 540 306.
dMehdi is the Director of DYAR.
d. Email
d[ICON: Envelope icon]
dSend an email to hello@dyar.com.au.
d. Letter or form
d[ICON: Pen and paper icon]
dWrite or draw what you want to say.
dUse our feedback form on the website.
dOr post it to: PO Box 8052, East Subiaco WA 6008.
d. Talk to your worker
d[ICON: Two people sitting together, one talking, one listening with caring expression]
dTell your support worker what you want to say.
dThey will pass it on.
dAsk someone to help
d[IMAGE: Three people holding hands ? a participant, a family member, and an advocate]
dYou can ask:
d-  Family or friends
d-  An advocate (see our Advocacy Flyer)
d-  Your support worker
d-  Someone you trust
dWhat happens next
d[IMAGE: Numbered step-by-step path with footprints ? 1, 2, 3, 4]
d. DYAR says thank you within 2 days.
d. DYAR looks at what you said.
d. DYAR tells you what we will do.
d. DYAR keeps you in the loop.
dIf you are not happy with DYAR's response
d[IMAGE: Person taking a complaint outside of one building to another (the NDIS Commission)]
dYou can talk to:
dNDIS Quality and Safeguards Commission
dWebsite: ndiscommission.gov.au
dThey keep an eye on all NDIS providers like DYAR.
dOr an advocate
dSee our Advocacy Flyer for free advocacy services.
dYou can be ANONYMOUS
d[ICON: Mask icon ? meaning private, no name needed]
dYou do NOT need to give your name.
dYou can use our Anonymous Complaint Form.
dDownload from dyar.com.au/feedback.
dDYAR Pty Ltd ? An NDIS Service Provider on Whadjuk Country ? Easy Read ? V2 19 May 2026

title: Join the DYAR Advisory Group (LIVE recruiting)
content: dJoin the DYAR Advisory Group
dPeople with disability shaping DYAR
d[HEADER-IMAGE: Diverse group of people with disability sitting around a table, smiling, with speech bubbles]
dWhat is this?
d[IMAGE: DYAR logo with a circle of supportive people around it]
dDYAR is starting an Advisory Group.
d to 5 people will join.
dThey will help DYAR get better.
dWho can join?
d[IMAGE: Four people of different ages, abilities, and cultural backgrounds smiling together]
dYOU ? if you have lived experience of disability.
dYour family member ? if they care for someone with disability.
dYou do NOT need a degree.
dYou do NOT need to be an expert.
dYou just need to bring your experience.
dWhat do members do?
d[IMAGE: Four meeting scenes ? one per quarter (Mar, Jun, Sep, Dec)]
dCome to 4 meetings a year.
dEach meeting is 2 hours.
dOnline or in our office in Osborne Park.
dTell DYAR what we are doing well.
dTell DYAR what we could do better.
dHelp DYAR check our forms and policies.
dWhat do you get?
d[IMAGE: Dollar bills + taxi + Auslan interpreter + Easy Read papers + certificate]
d$150 for each meeting.
dDYAR pays for your transport.
dDYAR pays for an Auslan interpreter if you need one.
dDYAR pays for someone to come with you if you want.
dDYAR will give papers in Easy Read.
dCertificate at the end of your term.
dYour voice changes how DYAR works.
dHow to join
d[IMAGE: Four icons: email, phone, advocate, web form]
dYou can:
d-  Email hello@dyar.com.au
d-  Call Mehdi: 0470 540 306
d-  Ask your support coordinator or advocate to call us
d-  Use the form on dyar.com.au/leag
dWhat to tell us
d[ICON: Speech bubble with simple list]
dJust tell us:
d-  Your name
d-  The best way to talk to you
d-  A bit about you
d-  What you want to share with DYAR
dYou can record it as a voice message or video. No writing needed.
dWhat happens next?
d[IMAGE: Path with 4 footstep marks numbered 1-2-3-4]
d. DYAR says hello within 2 days.
d. We have a 30-minute chat ? your way.
d. We say yes or thank-you.
d. First meeting in September 2026.
dEveryone is welcome
d[IMAGE: Wheelchair, Aboriginal flag, rainbow flag, hijab, hearing aid ? circle of diversity]
dDYAR welcomes all people.
dAboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people.
dPeople from any cultural background.
dLGBTIQA+ people.
dPeople of any age.
dPeople with any disability.
dDYAR Pty Ltd ? An NDIS Service Provider on Whadjuk Country ? Easy Read ? V1 19 May 2026

title: Incident Management
content: dWhat is an Incident?
d[HEADER-IMAGE: Person with a worried face next to a clipboard ? represents reporting an incident]
dAn incident is when something bad happens
d[IMAGE: Three icons: someone falling (injury), broken plate (damage), worried face (upset)]
dAn incident can be:
d-  Someone gets hurt
d-  Someone is unsafe
d-  Something gets broken
d-  Someone is treated badly
d-  A rule was not followed
dDYAR wants to know about ALL incidents. Even small ones.
dWhy DYAR wants to know
d[ICON: Light bulb above a person's head ? getting an idea]
dSo DYAR can help you feel better.
dSo DYAR can stop it happening again.
dSo DYAR can learn and do better.
dHow to tell DYAR
d[IMAGE: 4 ways: phone, email, paper form, in person]
dChoose what works for you:
d-  Call Mehdi: 0470 540 306
d-  Email: hello@dyar.com.au
d-  Tell your support worker
d-  Write on our Incident Report form
dSome incidents are very serious
d[IMAGE: Red exclamation mark in a triangle ? warning sign]
dThese are called REPORTABLE INCIDENTS.
dThey include:
d-  Serious injury
d-  Abuse or neglect
d-  Sexual misconduct
d-  Death
d-  Use of a restrictive practice without permission
dDYAR will tell the NDIS Commission about reportable incidents.
dWhat DYAR will do
d[IMAGE: Step-by-step numbered list with caring person walking through it]
d. Make sure you are safe.
d. Listen to what happened.
d. Help you if you are hurt.
d. Write down what happened.
d. Work out what to do next.
d. Tell you what we have done.
d. Check it does NOT happen again.
dYou are safe to speak up
d[IMAGE: Person standing tall with sun behind them ? safety + protection]
dYou will NOT get in trouble.
dYour supports will NOT stop.
dDYAR will help you feel okay again.
dYou can have someone with you
d[IMAGE: Two people standing together ? participant + supporter]
dYou can have:
d-  Family or friends
d-  An advocate
d-  A support worker
d-  Anyone you trust
dIf you don't want to use your name
d[ICON: Mask icon]
dThat is OK. You can be anonymous.
dUse our Anonymous Complaint Form.
dDownload at dyar.com.au/feedback.
dDYAR Pty Ltd ? An NDIS Service Provider on Whadjuk Country ? Easy Read ? V2 19 May 2026

title: Advocacy Flyer
content: dWho can speak up for you
d[HEADER-IMAGE: Friendly group of diverse people with disability and an advocate standing together, smiling, holding speech bubbles]
dWhat is an advocate?
d[IMAGE: Cartoon person with one hand on their chest and the other reaching up ? speaking confidently]
dAn advocate is a person who stands next to you.
dThey listen to what you want.
dThey help you say what you want.
dAdvocates do NOT work for DYAR
d[ICON: Two separate buildings with a person walking between them ? showing independence]
dAdvocates are independent.
dThey can help you, even if you have a problem with DYAR.
dAdvocates are FREE
d[ICON: Dollar sign with a red cross through it ? meaning free / no cost]
dYou do NOT need to pay anything.
dYou can call them yourself.
dOr DYAR can help you make the call.
dWA Advocacy Services
d[HEADER-IMAGE: Map of Western Australia with location pins]
dPeople With Disabilities WA (PWdWA)
d[ICON: Phone icon]
dFree call: 1800 193 331
dEmail: info@pwdwa.org
dWebsite: pwdwa.org
dBest for: All people with disability in WA.
dMIDLAS
d(Midland Information, Debt and Legal Advocacy Service)
dWebsite: midlas.org.au
dAddress: 12 Brockman Road, Midland WA 6056
dBest for: People in east and north-east Perth.
dDevelopmental Disability WA (DDWA)
d[ICON: Two people holding hands in a supportive way]
dWebsite: ddwa.org.au
dBest for: People with intellectual disability and their families.
dEthnic Disability Advocacy Centre (EDAC)
d[ICON: Earth globe with people of different colours around it]
dWebsite: edac.org.au
dBest for: People from culturally and linguistically diverse (CaLD) backgrounds.
dFirst Peoples Disability Network (FPDN)
d[ICON: Aboriginal flag colours (black, red, yellow) in a circle]
dWebsite: fpdn.org.au
dBest for: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people.
dMental Health Law Centre (MHLC)
d[ICON: Brain icon with a heart inside]
dWebsite: mhlcwa.org.au
dBest for: Free legal advice for mental health matters.
dHow DYAR can help
d[IMAGE: DYAR staff member sitting next to a participant, helping them make a phone call]
dYou can ask DYAR to:
d-  Find the right advocate for you.
d-  Make the first phone call with you.
d-  Translate or help with reading.
d-  Use Auslan or another language.
dContact DYAR
d[ICON: DYAR logo (a stylised D)]
dEmail: hello@dyar.com.au
dWebsite: dyar.com.au
dDYAR Pty Ltd ? An NDIS Service Provider on Whadjuk Country ? Easy Read ? V2 19 May 2026

title: Staying safe from COVID-19
content: dStaying safe from COVID-19
d[HEADER-IMAGE: Friendly cartoon COVID virus next to a person wearing a mask, with a hand-sanitiser bottle]
dWhat is COVID-19?
d[IMAGE: Microscope view of the COVID virus ? round with spikes]
dCOVID-19 is an illness.
dYou catch it from other people.
dIt spreads through coughs and sneezes.
dHow to stay safe
d[IMAGE: 4 icons: washing hands, wearing a mask, keeping distance, getting vaccinated]
d. Wash your hands
d[ICON: Soap + water + tap icon]
dWash with soap for 20 seconds.
dWash before you eat.
dWash when you come home.
d. Wear a mask if you are sick
d[ICON: Face mask icon]
dCover your mouth and nose.
dStay home if you feel sick.
d. Keep distance
d[ICON: Two people with a 1.5 metre arrow between them]
dStay 1.5 metres from people you don't live with.
dEspecially indoors.
d. Get vaccinated
d[ICON: Syringe / vaccine icon with a band-aid]
dVaccines protect you.
dThey are free.
dTalk to your doctor.
dIf you feel sick
d[IMAGE: Person with thermometer in mouth + tissue]
dStay home.
dCall your doctor.
dGet a test ? RAT or PCR.
dTell DYAR ? call 0470 540 306.
dIf you test positive
d[IMAGE: RAT test showing two lines + positive sign]
dStay home for 5 to 7 days.
dRest and drink water.
dTake medicine if your doctor says so.
dTell DYAR straight away.
dTell DYAR if you feel sick. DYAR will look after you. DYAR will not be cross.
dWhat DYAR does to keep you safe
d[IMAGE: Support worker washing hands + wearing a mask + cleaning surfaces]
dDYAR workers:
d-  Wash their hands often
d-  Wear a mask when needed
d-  Stay home if they feel sick
d-  Clean equipment between visits
dWhen DYAR will reschedule
d[IMAGE: Calendar with one date crossed out and another circled ? meaning rescheduled]
dDYAR will reschedule if:
d-  You are sick
d-  Your worker is sick
d-  Someone in the house is sick
dImportant numbers
d[ICON: Phone with contact list]
dDYAR ? Mehdi ? 0470 540 306
dHealthDirect ? 1800 022 222
dWA Health ? 13 COVID (13 26 843)
dYour doctor ? write here: ____________________
dDYAR Pty Ltd ? An NDIS Service Provider on Whadjuk Country ? Easy Read ? V2 19 May 2026

title: Child Safe
content: dDYAR keeps children safe
d[HEADER-IMAGE: Diverse children playing happily with a caring adult watching over them]
dChildren have rights
d[IMAGE: A child holding up a sign that says 'My rights matter']
dEvery child has the right to:
d-  Be safe
d-  Be listened to
d-  Be respected
d-  Be happy
d-  Have fun and play
d-  Speak up when something is wrong
dDYAR's promise to children
d[IMAGE: Two hands holding a heart with a child's drawing in the middle]
dDYAR will:
d-  Always listen to you
d-  Take you seriously
d-  Keep you safe
d-  Tell the truth
d-  Never hurt you
dOur workers
d[IMAGE: Support worker wearing a DYAR badge ? friendly smile + check-mark icon]
dEvery DYAR worker:
d-  Has a Working with Children Check
d-  Has training on keeping children safe
d-  Knows how to listen
d-  Will never hurt or scare you
dIf something is NOT okay
d[IMAGE: A child raising their hand ? like in class ? to speak up]
dIf someone:
d-  Hurts you
d-  Touches you in a way that feels wrong
d-  Says things that make you scared
d-  Tells you to keep secrets
d-  Makes you do something you don't want
dTELL someone you trust. Keep telling until someone helps.
dWho you can tell
d[IMAGE: Group of caring adults: parent, teacher, support worker, police]
dYou can tell:
d-  Mum, Dad, or your family
d-  A teacher
d-  A DYAR worker
d-  Mehdi at DYAR ? 0470 540 306
dOr call:
d-  Kids Helpline ? 1800 55 1800 (free)
d-  Police ? 000 if you are in danger
dThings DYAR will NEVER do
d[IMAGE: Red cross over: a worker giving alcohol, taking photos, asking for secrets, hitting]
dDYAR workers will NEVER:
d-  Hit, push, or grab you
d-  Yell or call you names
d-  Touch you in a way that feels wrong
d-  Ask you to keep secrets from your parents
d-  Take photos without permission
d-  Give you alcohol or drugs
d-  Take you somewhere your parents do not know
dYou are special
d[IMAGE: Star surrounded by sparkles + a smiling child]
dDYAR is happy you are here.
dYour voice matters.
dYou belong.
dDYAR Pty Ltd ? An NDIS Service Provider on Whadjuk Country ? Easy Read ? V2 19 May 2026

title: What to do in an Emergency
content: dWhat to do in an Emergency
d[HEADER-IMAGE: Bright red exit sign with a person running towards it]
dIf you are in danger ? call 000
d[IMAGE: Big red 000 numbers with a phone icon]
d is for:
d-  Police
d-  Ambulance
d-  Fire
dCall 000 if anyone is hurt, in danger, or there is a fire.
dIf there is a FIRE
d[IMAGE: Flame icon with arrow showing the way to safety]
d. Tell everyone to GET OUT.
d. Close doors behind you if you can.
d. Go to the meeting place outside.
d. Call 000.
d. Do NOT go back inside.
dDYAR meeting points
d[IMAGE: Map showing the office building (La Villa Centro) with a green meeting-point flag on Main Street footpath]
dAt DYAR Office
dMeeting place: Main Street footpath outside La Villa Centro.
dAt your home
dYour worker will agree a meeting place with you.
dIt is usually the letterbox or driveway.
dIf there is a MEDICAL emergency
d[IMAGE: Red cross / first-aid kit icon next to a person on the ground]
dStay calm.
dCall 000 and ask for an ambulance.
dTell them the address.
dStay with the person until help comes.
dIf there is a BUSHFIRE warning
d[IMAGE: Fire-danger sign with the levels Low / Moderate / High / Extreme / Catastrophic]
dCheck the WA Emergency website: emergency.wa.gov.au.
dIf the danger is HIGH or higher ? leave early.
dDYAR will call you to check you are safe.
dIf there is a BIG STORM
d[IMAGE: Storm cloud with lightning and rain]
dStay inside.
dStay away from windows.
dHave a torch and a phone charged.
dIf the POWER goes out
d[IMAGE: Power outlet with a red cross + a torch icon]
dUse a torch ? NOT candles.
dPhone Mehdi: 0470 540 306.
dIf you have equipment that needs power ? call us straight away.
dDYAR is here to help
d[IMAGE: Friendly support worker with phone in one hand and reaching out with the other]
dIn any emergency, you can call DYAR.
dPhone Mehdi: 0470 540 306.
dEmail: hello@dyar.com.au.
dWe answer day and night for urgent things.
dImportant numbers
d[ICON: Phone book / contact list icon]
dPolice, Fire, Ambulance ? 000
dPolice (non-emergency) ? 131 444
dMental Health Emergency Response Line ? 1300 555 788
dLifeline (sad or scared) ? 13 11 14
dPoisons Information ? 13 11 26
dDYAR ? Mehdi ? 0470 540 306
dDYAR Pty Ltd ? An NDIS Service Provider on Whadjuk Country ? Easy Read ? V2 19 May 2026

title: Thriving Kids briefing for families
content: dThriving Kids ? what's changing
d[HEADER-IMAGE: Smiling group of children with disabilities playing ? a calendar showing October 2026 + January 2028 highlighted]
dWhat is Thriving Kids?
d[IMAGE: Sunflower / growing-up symbol ? a small plant turning into a tall flower]
dThriving Kids is a new way the government helps young children with disability.
dIt is also called Foundational Supports.
dIt is for kids from 0 to 8 years old.
dIt is for kids with low to moderate needs.
dWhen does it start?
d[IMAGE: Calendar with two dates circled: 1 October 2026 + 1 January 2028]
dIt starts on 1 October 2026.
dIt will be fully running by 1 January 2028.
dWho is it for?
d[IMAGE: Child icon (age 5) with arrows pointing to: speech therapy, OT, group activities]
dChildren who:
d-  Are 0 to 8 years old
d-  Have low to moderate needs
d-  Need help with talking, moving, or learning
d-  Need help getting ready for school
dWill my child still get NDIS?
d[IMAGE: Two paths splitting ? one labelled NDIS, one labelled Thriving Kids ? caring parent looking at both]
dSome children will go to Thriving Kids.
dSome children will stay on NDIS.
dChildren with HIGH needs will stay on NDIS.
dChildren with LOW or MODERATE needs may move to Thriving Kids.
dNothing changes for your child today. The change starts October 2026.
dWhat is DYAR doing?
d[IMAGE: DYAR worker reading a roadmap with stars on it ? preparing]
dDYAR is getting ready.
dWe will keep helping your child with NDIS.
dWe will be ready for Thriving Kids too.
dWe will talk to you well before any change.
dWhat do I need to do now?
d[IMAGE: Checklist with 3 ticks]
d. Keep using your child's NDIS supports.
d. Ask us questions.
d. Tell DYAR if your child's needs change.
dWhere to find more information
d[IMAGE: Computer + phone + brochure ? multiple ways to get information]
dNDIS website: ndis.gov.au/thriving-kids
dFoundational Supports info: dss.gov.au
dOr call DYAR: 0470 540 306
dYou are not alone
d[IMAGE: Family holding hands with their child + DYAR worker walking alongside]
dDYAR will walk with you through every change.
dAsk anything. We are here to help.
dDYAR Pty Ltd ? An NDIS Service Provider on Whadjuk Country ? Easy Read ? V1 19 May 2026
